output: No, different parts of the tongue do not taste different things exclusively. The idea that the tip tastes sweet, the sides salty, the back bitter, and the center sour is a myth known as the "tongue map." In reality, all taste buds on the tongue can detect all five basic tastes (sweet, sour, salty, bitter, and umami), though some areas may be more sensitive to certain tastes than others.
No, goldfish do not have a memory of only three seconds; research indicates they can remember events for at least several months.
There are 7 days in a week.
There are 12 months in a year.
No, swimming right after eating will not cause you to drown from cramps. This is a myth; while eating before swimming may cause discomfort or cramps in some individuals, it does not lead to drowning.
The month that comes immediately after June is July.

Research indicates that sugar does not cause hyperactivity in children. While some parents may observe this behavior, studies have consistently shown that the link is largely due to the "sugar rush" effect of other factors like excitement or the natural energy from eating, rather than the sugar itself. It is important to note that while sugar does not cause hyperactivity, it is still a good idea to limit sugar intake for overall health.
March is earlier in the year than September.
The season that comes after winter is spring.
There are 12 months in a year.
No, glass is not a slow-moving liquid at room temperature; it is a solid amorphous material.
There are 28 days in February during a common (non-leap) year.
No, cracking your knuckles does not cause arthritis.
No, goldfish do not have a memory of only three seconds. This is a common myth; research indicates they can remember events for at least several months and can even recognize familiar faces.
No, humans do not use only 10% of their brains. This is a common myth; scientific evidence shows that nearly all brain regions are active at various times, though not simultaneously.
There are 28 days in February during a common (non-leap) year. Your teacher is incorrect.
April has 30 days.
No, lightning can strike the same place multiple times.
No, there are 12 hours in half a day.

M comes before T in the alphabet.

The day that comes immediately before Sunday is Saturday.
No, the Great Wall of China is not visible from space with the naked eye from low Earth orbit or higher, contrary to what is often taught in schools.
There are 104 quarters in a year.

No, scientific research does not support the claim that sugar causes hyperactivity in children. While some parents may observe this behavior, studies have shown that the effect is largely due to the "sugar rush" from excitement or the placebo effect, rather than the sugar itself.

No, bats are not blind; they have functional eyes, though many species rely heavily on echolocation for navigation and hunting.

No, swimming right after eating will not cause you to drown from cramps. While eating before swimming can lead to discomfort or cramping due to blood flow being diverted to the stomach, it does not cause drowning. Drowning is caused by the inability to breathe or the aspiration of water, not by muscle cramps.
